microsoft_ft: Scaled Azure's **infrastructure reliability** program from a 2-person operation into a platformized system, standardizing workflows to sustain 45+ annual resilience drills and drive **$14M+ business impact** across enterprise partners.

Led 0→1 delivery of an internal **Resilience Automation Platform**, defining self-service scheduling requirements that reduced operational toil by 30% and freed engineering capacity for higher-priority infrastructure work.

Pioneered Azure's first proactive rack-level resilience testing capability, delivering the program in **4 months** at a 94% recovery rate, surfacing critical hardware defects and establishing a continuous validation model.

Directed **14 cross-org recovery executions** under executive visibility, serving as bridge lead for a sovereign-cloud network isolation test tied to a $1.5B+ enterprise contract requiring strict security and reliability standards.

Built an internal **AI agent** to automate drill planning workflows, cutting planning cycle time by 39% and increasing drill capacity by 21%—directly advancing AI-assisted engineering productivity across the infrastructure org.

Established reliability metrics and operational dashboards tracking infrastructure health and capacity utilization, enabling engineering leadership to **drive alignment** on priorities and surface systemic risk proactively.
microsoft_2023: Drove **AI tool adoption** across 14 Azure engineering teams by leading hands-on demos and training sessions for AI-driven code generation workflows, saving 37 engineering hours monthly and improving developer productivity.

Translated research-team needs into concrete roadmap requirements by facilitating **11+ user interviews** with Azure service teams, securing product commitment to intent-based YAML generation to close critical feature gaps.

Improved **developer productivity** by implementing metadata standards and migrating documentation to an AI-powered semantic search platform, cutting engineering lookup time by 83% and reducing friction in daily workflows.
microsoft_2022: Generated **$3M in accelerated revenue** and launched Azure regions 28% faster by driving cross-functional alignment on a unified automation prioritization framework spanning 140+ engineering and infrastructure teams.

Defined a platform strategy to eliminate operational toil during region launches, conducting discovery with 20+ service teams to surface **81 hours of manual effort** per region and building a roadmap to automate critical paths.

Enabled leadership to target **automation gaps** and prioritize high-impact infrastructure improvements by engineering a Power BI dashboard tracking operational toil and capacity utilization across 140+ teams.
amazon_robotics: Achieved **zero operational downtime** during a 2,000+ unit pilot by defining the legacy OS migration strategy, mapping dependencies across 1,200+ stations, and coordinating cross-functional execution across IT and Engineering.

Facilitated Agile ceremonies including sprint planning and retrospectives, prioritizing the backlog to resolve **40+ high-priority tickets** and stabilize the developer intake process for the infrastructure engineering team.

Drove alignment across IT, Operations, and Engineering to implement **automated CI/CD pipelines**, accelerating the software deployment cycle by 25% and reducing manual release toil across the robotics infrastructure stack.
pro_painters: Increased job bookings by **26%** by optimizing end-to-end operations and managing the full scoping and invoicing lifecycle for 200+ monthly proposals through a redesigned CRM process that reduced execution bottlenecks.

Reduced **Customer Acquisition Cost by 13%** and improved conversion rates by 2.7% by executing a data-driven digital-first go-to-market strategy, optimizing web presence and improving funnel performance metrics.

Improved leadership pricing models by conducting **financial profitability analysis** across 1,000+ project records, identifying and sealing margin leaks to drive measurable improvements in overall business unit performance.

tailoring_notes: 'Infrastructure reliability' and 'reliability targets' from the JD woven into microsoft_ft bullets #1 and #6 — framing Azure resilience work as reliability program ownership.
'Developer productivity' and 'AI tools into development workflows' from the JD woven into microsoft_2023 bullets #1 and #3 — directly mirroring the JD's Developer Productivity & Tooling section.
'CI/CD pipelines' and 'automated CI/CD' from the JD woven into amazon_robotics bullet #3 — matching the JD's explicit call-out of CI/CD infrastructure experience.

'Operational toil', 'capacity utilization', and 'operational dashboards' from the JD's Infrastructure Reliability & Operations section woven into microsoft_ft bullet #6 and microsoft_2022 bullets #2 and #3.

'Drive alignment on priorities' and 'cross-functional coordination' from the JD woven into microsoft_ft bullet #4 and microsoft_2022 bullet #1 — mirroring the JD's Cross-functional Coordination section language.
